     The Numbers Business Report

Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $291,997,603Details
International Box Office $594,776,557
Worldwide Box Office $886,774,160
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $222,410,787
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,237,064
Total $228,647,851
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It’s Harry Potter’s fourth term at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry! Harry, Ron and Hermione look forward to the international Quidditch finals. Harry dreams of spending time with the alluring Cho Chang. He wants to be a normal 14-year-old wizard . . . But Harry is not normal–even by wizarding standards. This term, for the first time in hundreds of years, a competition will be held between three schools of wizardry, and contestants will be magically chosen for the very dangerous event. Now, as Harry’s lightning scar burns, indicating the evil presence of Lord Voldemort, the Goblet of Fire makes its final selection for the competition–Harry Potter.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$102,685,961 (35.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.84 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$150,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,858 opening theaters/3,858 max. theaters, 7.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $541,912,754

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 18th, 2005 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
November 18th, 2005 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
September 2nd, 2018 (Limited), released as Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire (2005) (Re-Release)
February 16th, 2025 (Special Engagement) by Fathom Events
International Releases: November 18th, 2005 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
November 18th, 2005 (Wide), released as Harry Potter ve Ateş Kadehi (Turkey)
November 18th, 2005 (Wide), released as Harry Potter y el cáliz de fuego (Mexico)
November 24th, 2005 (Wide) (New Zealand)
November 25th, 2005 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: March 7th, 2006 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of fantasy violence and frightening images.
Running Time: 150 minutes
Franchise: Harry Potter
Cast, crew, or production detail: Visual Effects
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Social setting: Boarding School
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Heyday Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

It’s Harry Potter’s fourth term at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry! Harry, Ron and Hermione look forward to the international Quidditch finals. Harry dreams of spending time with the alluring Cho Chang. He wants to be a normal 14-year-old wizard . . . But Harry is not normal–even by wizarding standards. This term, for the first time in hundreds of years, a competition will be held between three schools of wizardry, and contestants will be magically chosen for the very dangerous event. Now, as Harry’s lightning scar burns, indicating the evil presence of Lord Voldemort, the Goblet of Fire makes its final selection for the competition–Harry Potter.
